events.event_description: Joel Roberts Poinsett presents credentials as Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary, establishing the U.S. Legation in Mexico.

evidence.quote: Joel Roberts Poinsett, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1825-03-08. Credentials presented: 1825-06-01.

events.event_description: Mexico severs diplomatic relations with the United States, ending the U.S. Legation.

evidence.quote: Mexico severed diplomatic relations with the United States on 1845-03-28. Note: Shannon left post on May 14, 1845.

events.event_description: Nathan Clifford presents credentials as Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary, restoring the U.S. Legation in Mexico after the break in relations.

evidence.quote: Nathan Clifford, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1848-07-28. Credentials presented: 1848-10-02. Presented recall on 1849-09-06.

events.event_description: Envoy Forsyth suspends political relations of the U.S. Legation with the Government of Mexico.

evidence.quote: John2 Forsyth, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1856-07-21. Credentials presented: 1856-10-23. Suspended political relations of the Legation with Government of Mexico on 1858-06-21.

events.event_description: Robert Milligan McLane presents credentials as Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary, restoring the U.S. Legation in Mexico.

evidence.quote: Robert Milligan Mclane, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1859-03-07. Credentials presented: 1859-04-06. Presented recall on 1860-12-22.

events.event_description: The U.S. Legation in Mexico is elevated to an Embassy when Powell Clayton is promoted to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary.

evidence.quote: Powell Clayton, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1897-03-22. Credentials presented: 1897-05-13. Promoted to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary 1899-01-13.

events.event_description: The U.S. Embassy in Mexico City is closed at the request of Mexican authorities, ending formal diplomatic representation.

evidence.quote: Nelson Oshaugnessy, Chargé d'Affaires ad interim. Embassy Mexico City closed at the request of the Mexican authorities on 1914-04-22.

events.event_description: Henry Prather Fletcher presents credentials as Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary, reopening the U.S. Embassy in Mexico.

evidence.quote: Henry Prather Fletcher, Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1916-02-25. Credentials presented: 1917-03-03. Left post on 1919-01-25.
